title: A Stable Right-Sided Quaternion Fourier Regime for Multichannel Spectral Analysis
abstract: This paper defines a stable right-sided discrete quaternion Fourier regime for multichannel spectral analysis. Building on a canonical quaternion lane representation of paired complex channels, it treats each lifted lane as a quaternion-valued sequence x[n] in H and adopts a fixed transform axis i. The forward transform is X[k]=sum_{n=0}^{N-1} x[n] exp(-i 2πnk/N), with inverse x[n]=(1/N)sum_{k=0}^{N-1} X[k] exp(+i 2πnk/N). The paper proves exact inversion, right-linearity over the commuting slice C_i, nonnegativity of spectral power, and Parseval invariance. The scope is intentionally limited to a one-dimensional fixed-axis right-sided regime intended as a deterministic spectral seam for quaternion-lifted multichannel processing.
notes: Upgraded from scaffold-only package to a full manuscript draft with formal transform definition, inversion, right-linearity, spectral power nonnegativity, and Parseval invariants for lanewise multichannel extension.
